'''Shannon Wakeman''' (born 15 February 1990), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''TS Wakeman''', is an [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last contracted to the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He plays as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Huddersfield Giants sign Australian/Italian prop Shannon Wakeman on two-year contract">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/10704833/huddersfield-giants-sign-australian-prop-shannon-wakeman-on-two-year-contract|title=Huddersfield Giants sign Australian prop Shannon Wakeman on two-year contract|publisher=Sky Sports|date=22 December 2016|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref>

==Italy==
Wakeman is an [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] international due to having Italian Grandparents.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/wcq/report?RLE00000700|title=Italy 76 - 0 Russia|publisher=RLEF|date=4 November 2016|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref>

==Background==
He played his junior rugby league for the [[Dapto Canaries]].<ref name="NRL Lowdown Round 18: Who’s on the cusp and who’s on the block at your club?">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-lowdown-round-18-whos-on-the-cusp-and-whos-on-the-block-at-your-club/news-story/cd6facd2341b702e2dd6438bc7476247|title=NRL Lowdown Round 18: Who's on the cusp and who's on the block at your club?|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=7 July 2015|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref> Wakeman played for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] side for two seasons between 2009 and 2010.<ref name="Cutters duo join Dragons for 2013">{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/cutters-duo-join-dragons-2013-8037/|title=Cutters duo join Dragons for 2013|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=30 October 2012|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref>

In 2012 he played for the [[Windsor Wolves]] in the [[New South Wales Cup|NSW Cup]].<ref name="Player Profile">{{cite web|url=http://websites.sportstg.com/team_info.cgi?action=PSTATS&pID=194435495&client=1-8908-125915-404231-25109503|title=Player Profile|publisher=Sports TG|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref> He had been with the [[Penrith Panthers]] feeder club since 2011.<ref name="Cutters duo join Dragons for 2013">{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/cutters-duo-join-dragons-2013-8037/|title=Cutters duo join Dragons for 2013|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=30 October 2012|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref>

He then moved on to play for the [[Illawarra Cutters]] in the [[New South Wales Cup]] from 2013.<ref name="Dragons recruit ready to hit his peak">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/dragons-recruit-ready-to-hit-his-peak/tabid/10874/newsid/83066/default.aspx?cid=NRL_HP_Latest|title=Dragons recruit ready to hit his peak|publisher=NRL|date=18 November 2014|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref>
Wakeman signed with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[NRL]], making the step up from feeder club to the elite level.<ref name="Shannon Wakeman ready to deliver">{{cite web|url=http://www.illawarramercury.com.au/story/2665520/shannon-wakeman-ready-to-deliver/|title=Shannon Wakeman ready to deliver|publisher=Illawarra Mercury|date=31 October 2014|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref> Whilst he failed to make a first-grade appearance in the NRL, he did attract headlines for his performances for the [[Illawarra Cutters|Cutters]] in the [[Intrust Super Premiership]].<ref name="Shannon Wakeman - 2016 ISP NSW TOTY">{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/10/28/shannon_wakeman_2016.html|title=Shannon Wakeman - 2016 ISP NSW TOTY|publisher=NSWRL|date=28 October 2016|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref> Wakeman was a member of Illawarra's 2016 premiership winning side and also a member and first try scorer of the Illawarra side which won the NRL State Championship match in 2016 against The [[Burleigh Bears]].<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/illawarra-cutters-flog-burleigh-bears-in-interstate-championship-decider-20161002-grtboa.html | title=Illawarra Cutters flog Burleigh Bears in Interstate Championship decider | first=Mitchell | last=Jennings | newspaper=Sydney Morning Herald | date=2 October 2016 | accessdate=27 March 2018 }}</ref>

==Super League Break==
In late December 2016 he signed a two-year full-time contract to play for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Shannon Wakeman: Huddersfield Giants sign Australian prop on two-year deal">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38403563|title=Shannon Wakeman: Huddersfield Giants sign Australian prop on two-year deal|publisher=BBC|date=22 December 2016|accessdate=22 December 2016}}</ref> He was coached by former [[Newcastle Knights]] boss, [[Rick Stone]].

==Controversy==
In October 2017 while playing for Italy in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]], Wakeman allegedly [[king hit]] teammate [[James Tedesco]] while having drinks at The Pier Bar in [[Cairns]] following Italy's shock 36–12 defeat by Ireland. The altercation was reportedly due to a misunderstanding with Wakeman's girlfriend.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/james-tedesco-king-hit-in-cairns-nightspot-report-20171031-gzbuar.html/|title=James Tedesco king hit in Cairns nightspot|website=SMH}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/world-cup/nrl-star-james-tedesco-king-hit-by-italy-teammate-shannon-wakeman-media-watch/news-story/f497981ad900eafe064bcb2ba9b7d37b/|title=NRL star James Tedesco king hit by Italy teammate Shannon Wakeman|website=Fox Sports}}</ref>
